creator: kabe@sra-tohoku.co.jp
text:
Dell Latitude XPi sporting TI1131 CardBus bridge.

Any PC card hotplugged is not detected; seems FreeBSD cannot detect 
card insertion event.
When inserted befor boot, dmesg seems to detect the card existence,
but still the card as a device is not initialized as a device,
which is still not usable.

Since this machine needed IRQ quirks on other OS (QNX) to use
PC card properly, the cause may be IRQ routing problem.
It is unknown whether this is the TI1131 problem or
this particular laptop's problem.

First, the list below is "/boot/loader.conf", "atacontrol list", 
"dmesg" and "sysctl -a"
output, when booted with a PC card (CompactFlash ATA card via PC Card
adapter) inserted before boot.

The dmesg doesn't initialize "ad4" disk device under ATA channel 2, 
even after the existence of the device is detected as "pqi Flash Card".

======================= /boot/loader.conf
# this HDD doesn't have DMA?
hw.ata.ata_dma=0

# no device.hints needed; defaults to port 0x61
speaker_load="YES"		# speaker(4); AT speaker module

# TI1131 doesn't route IRQ properly?
hw.cardbus.debug=1
hw.pccard.debug=1
hw.cbb.debug=1

======================= atacontrol.ins
ATA channel 0:
    Master:  ad0 <TOSHIBA THNCF128MCA/3.00> ATA/ATAPI revision 0
    Slave:       no device present
ATA channel 1:
    Master: acd0 <CD-ROM CDR N110D/1.05> ATA/ATAPI revision 0
    Slave:       no device present
ATA channel 2:
    Master:      no device present
    Slave:       no device present
